title: XML parsing vulnerable to entity expansion attacks
description:
File: python/src/lichen/gateway/compact_cot.py
Line: 687

The parse_cot_xml() function uses xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring() to parse potentially untrusted XML from external ATAK systems. While Python's ElementTree doesn't expand external entities, it IS vulnerable to:

1. Billion Laughs attack (exponential entity expansion)
2. Quadratic Blowup attack (polynomial memory consumption)

Both can cause denial of service by exhausting memory.

Fix: Use defusedxml.ElementTree instead:
```python
from defusedxml.ElementTree import fromstring
```

Or configure a safe parser:
```python
from xml.etree.ElementTree import XMLParser, fromstring
parser = XMLParser()
parser.entity = {}  # Disable entity expansion
```

Severity: High - Gateway receives XML from external ATAK clients over the network.
close_reason: Fixed: Replaced xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring with defusedxml.ElementTree.fromstring in parse_cot_xml() to prevent XML entity expansion attacks (Billion Laughs, Quadratic Blowup, XXE). Added defusedxml dependency and 5 security tests.
